response: The internet originated from 1960s U.S. Department of Defense computer networking research, beginning with the 1969 demonstration of precursor network ARPANET, before the 1983 transition to TCP/IP protocols established the modern internet's technical foundation; Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989, and the 1993 launch of the Mosaic browser made the technology accessible to ordinary users. Following the late 1990s dot-com boom and 2001 market crash that left enduring industry leaders including Amazon and Google, subsequent major developments included the rise of mid-2000s social media, the 2006 launch of AWS cloud computing, and the 2007 iPhone release which popularized ubiquitous mobile internet. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people worldwide, and underpins nearly every facet of modern commerce, communication, and daily life.
